heroHeadline: Af-Pak Non-Escalation Holds Day 14; Baloch Militants Kill 10 at Chagai Copper Mine; Pakistan Deports 146,000+ Afghans
heroSubtitle: The Afghanistan-Pakistan non-escalation framework reached its 14th consecutive day on April 23 without major cross-border military incidents — the longest unbroken lull since Operation Ghazab-il-Haq began in February 2026, with Urumqi Round 2 on track for mid-to-late May. Separately, approximately 40 Baloch militants stormed a copper/gold mine in Chagai district, Balochistan, killing 10 people including a Turkish national — the latest attack in an ongoing Baloch separatist campaign that has killed 200+ in 2026. Human Rights Watch documented a surge in Pakistan's forced deportation of Afghan refugees, with 146,000+ expelled from Pakistan in 2026 alone under conditions the rights group says violate international law, including forced returns of journalists, activists, and former Afghan government officials.
footerNote: Data sourced from UNAMA, SIGAR, ACLED, Brown University Costs of War Project, US DoD, Reuters, BBC, Tolo News, Dawn, Al Jazeera, HRW, OHCHR
